data.title: Varaha and Microsoft sign agreement for 100,000+ tonnes of carbon dioxide removal in 3 years.
data.description: Varaha, a prominent company specializing in carbon dioxide removal (CDR) projects with smallholder farmers in Asia, has recently unveiled a significant development. The firm disclosed an agreement that entails the removal of more than 100,000 tonnes of carbon dioxide over the span of three years. This collaboration is expected to have a notable impact on carbon offsetting initiatives in the region.

data.title: Progress report on Calcatreu Project: Construction advancing rapidly with investments exceeding $100 million.
data.description: Nearly 1,500 new COVID-19 cases were reported in the country in a single day, bringing the total number of cases to over 75,000. The death toll also rose by 30, with the total number of fatalities now reaching 2,500. The government has announced new restrictions in an effort to curb the spread of the virus, including the closure of non-essential businesses and a limit on public gatherings. Officials are urging the public to follow safety guidelines and get vaccinated to help combat the ongoing surge in cases.

data.title: Analysts discuss if Bitcoin could mimic gold's surge or face greater volatility due to market differences.
data.description: Financial analysts are currently divided on the future trajectory of Bitcoin, debating whether the cryptocurrency will experience a surge similar to gold driven by scarcity or if it will follow a more volatile path due to differences in market structure. With Bitcoin currently valued at approximately $60,000 per coin and gold trading at around $1,800 per ounce, the comparison between the two assets remains a hot topic of discussion among experts in the financial industry. The contrasting views stem from the unique characteristics of Bitcoin, such as its digital nature and limited supply, which set it apart from traditional commodities like gold.

data.title: TNG Digital and EPOS launch EPOS360 in Malaysia to aid SMEs with AI and payment tools. CEO MDEC supports initiative.
data.description: In Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, TNG Digital Sdn. Bhd. introduced EPOS360 in partnership with Ant International's EPOS, providing innovative AI-driven tools and payment features to aid in the digitization of businesses in Malaysia. This launch aims to support SMEs in the country. The app TNG eWallet, known for digital financial services and lifestyle features, will be integral to this collaboration. Anuar Fariz Fadzil, CEO of MDEC, expressed enthusiasm for this initiative.

data.title: MEXC reports 2025 growth over 130%, saving users $1B+ with zero-fee trading. Fastest-growing global crypto exchange.
data.description: In Victoria, Seychelles, on January 15, 2026, MEXC, the rapidly expanding worldwide cryptocurrency exchange, which prioritizes users and offers zero-fee trading, unveiled its 2025 Annual Report. This report highlights a year focused on deliberate strategies aimed at improving user experience and expanding their user base.

data.title: LUNGevity & Rising Tide Foundation offer up to $1 million for research on lung cancer drug resistance and recurrence.
data.description: Research projects that can make a tangible difference for patients are eligible for funding of up to $1 million each. In a recent development on January 15, 2026, LUNGevity Foundation, a prominent non-profit organization dedicated to lung cancer, along with Rising Tide Foundation for Clinical Cancer Research, announced this initiative.

data.title: Tech stocks rebounded, with S&P 500 & Nasdaq 100 futures up. Taiwan Semiconductor reports 35% profit surge for Q4. Goldman Sachs to report $13.79 billion in revenue. Markets expect Federal Reserve to keep interest rates unchanged. Dow Jones +0.11%, S&P 500 +0.36%, Nasdaq 100 +0.75%.
data.description: U.S. stock futures rose after a decline, with major benchmark indices higher. Nasdaq fell by 1% due to reports of a 25% tariff on select semiconductor imports. 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.15%, and the two-year bond was at 3.52%. FedWatch tool shows a 95% likelihood of the Federal Reserve maintaining current interest rates in January. Index performances showed Dow Jones +0.11%, S&P 500 +0.36%, Nasdaq 100 +0.75%, Russell 2000 +0.16%. SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF were up in premarkets. Stocks in focus: Goldman Sachs expected to report $11.65 per share earnings and $13.79 billion in revenue; Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing gained 4.46% after reporting a 35% profit surge in Q4 to T$505.7 billion ($16 billion).
